answer:
The maximum loss of kinetic energy in a one-dimensional collision occurs when the collision is perfectly inelastic, meaning the coefficient of restitution is zero and the particles stick together. Using conservation of momentum, \( m u_1 = (m + 2m) v \), gives \( v = \frac{1}{3} u_1 \). Thus, the velocity of the first particle after collision is \( \frac{1}{3} u_1 \).

\boxed{B}
